members.name: Courtney Lee
members.shortBio: Courtney leads Space Heard Us with a heart for inclusion, bringing music to people who need joy, welcome, and connection.
members.extendedStory: Courtney's life has been deeply shaped by the special needs community through her own family. She founded Space Heard Us to serve the community and to shine a light on the unique gifts and talents of children with disabilities.

members.name: Kaden Joo
members.shortBio: Kaden lives with autism and a hearing difference, and he expresses himself most fluently at the piano.
members.extendedStory: Kaden lives with autism and a hearing difference, yet when he sits down at the piano, barriers disappear. Music is his language: precise, expressive, and entirely his own. His commitment to practice and performance is a testament to what dedication and passion can achieve. Kaden is not defined by what the world assumes about him; he defines himself through the music he plays. For families of children with disabilities, watching Kaden perform is a powerful reminder that our kids contain multitudes we are only beginning to discover.

members.name: Eric Kim
members.shortBio: Eric lives with autism and intellectual disability, and he chooses to sing with warmth, courage, and joy.
members.extendedStory: Eric lives with autism and intellectual disability. He could have stayed quiet, but instead he sings. Every time Eric steps up to perform, he is making a statement: that his voice matters, that he belongs on that stage, and that the world is richer for hearing him. His warmth and presence are immediately felt by every audience. Eric is not performing despite his challenges; he is performing because of who he is, fully and without apology. For parents who wonder what their child's future might look like, Eric is an answer full of possibility.
